published_prior_art.cite: ma2021minimaxope + Le Cam/OPE converse template, but not adaptive Neyman-regret second-moment paths
neopane2024logarithmic + Neyman loss and oracle allocation definitions already imply the algebraic expansion
kato2025treatmentchoice + change-of-measure lower bounds for treatment-choice simple regret, not cumulative Neyman-regret variance inefficiency
neopane2024logarithmic and noarov2025stronger give O(log T) or near-log upper bounds, not an inf_Alg sup_nu Omega(log T) minimax converse
neopane2024logarithmic + balanced oracle allocation when arm root second moments coincide
zhao2023adaptive + standard-deviation learning motivation, but no closed-form cumulative Neyman-regret complexity frontier

novelty_flags.label: thm:global-log-rate
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship lower-bound claim cites Neopane/Noarov by paper-level bibkey only and gives no theorem, section, equation, or quoted open-problem anchor for the missing converse.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / thm:instance-local-minimax
novelty_flags.one_line: Zhao (2023) is a closer adaptive Neyman-allocation standard-deviation-learning comparator than the prose acknowledges and should be positioned explicitly.

novelty_flags.label: comparator_promise_table:neopane2024logarithmic
novelty_flags.one_line: The table labels thm:global-log-rate as an iff_frontier match, but the statement is a one-sided minimax lower bound paired only informally with a published upper bound.

soundness_flags.label: thm:instance-local-minimax / lem:path-existence
soundness_flags.one_line: M_int allows degenerate or two-point arm laws where mean-preserving L2-score perturbations of the second moment may be unavailable, making J, h_T, and the claimed path construction ill-posed without a nondegenerate tangent condition.

soundness_flags.label: thm:instance-local-minimax
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem needs an explicit finite positive kappa_nu and nonempty feasible-direction condition; as written kappa_nu may be 0 from an empty supremum or infinite, while the statement still quantifies an approximating direction and h_T.

soundness_flags.label: prop:symmetric-collapse
soundness_flags.one_line: m_0=m_1 gives pi_nu_star=1/2 and zero regret for the fixed balanced design at the base law, but it does not imply kappa_nu=0 because asymmetric second-moment directions have nonzero dot_pi_nu.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:closed-form-frontier
soundness_flags.one_line: kappa_nu is defined instancewise for a fixed nu, so asking whether the bounded-outcome class attains kappa_nu at an extremal law is missing the outer optimization or class-level frontier object.

soundness_flags.label: ass:interior-second-moments
soundness_flags.one_line: Neyman (1934) supports classical allocation by standard deviations, not this modern uncentered-second-moment HT interior condition; cite Dai/Neopane or retag the condition.

soundness_flags.label: ass:interior-band
soundness_flags.one_line: Noarov et al. (2025) is a finite-population upper-bound comparator and does not by itself establish a superpopulation root-second-moment band as a standard named assumption.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:closed-form-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The constant-exact closed-form frontier and extremal-law solution are open and not assumed by the log-rate lower-bound headline.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The main novelty is only the conditional Omega(log T) lower-bound route with kappa_nu left as an information-program handle.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a primitive nondegenerate tangent/rank assumption for each arm, e.g. positive residual variance of y^2 after projection on span{1,y}, and thread it through lem:path-existence and thm:instance-local-minimax.

Replace prop:symmetric-collapse with the correct boundary statement: balanced fixed design has zero regret at the symmetric base law, while local asymmetric second-moment alternatives can still yield positive kappa_nu.
Anchor Neopane/Noarov with exact theorem or section locations for their O(log T) upper bounds and state the lower-bound gap as a rate-matching converse, not an iff frontier.

Redefine the closed-form-frontier OEQ as a class-level optimization, e.g. sup or inf over laws with fixed primitive moment bands, or drop the extremal-law language and ask for a closed-form expression for instancewise kappa_nu under the new tangent regularity.

recommended_next_step: Revise this angle before Stage 0: fix the tangent regularity, symmetric-collapse sanity check, and flagship comparator anchoring.
